description: This add-on controls the foreground Windows application using the Computer Use tool based on a provided prompt. It captures the active window, sends it to the configured model, and performs mouse and keyboard actions.

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis:
Package: annotated-types (whl)
Version: 0.7.0
Description: Reusable constraint types to use with typing.Annotated

The provided source code is for an NVDA (NonVisual Desktop Access) add-on called 'Computer Use' that enables an AI model to interact with the user's computer. It bundles several standard Python libraries, including the OpenAI Python SDK, Pydantic, HTTPX, and others. The add-on includes functional modules for capturing screenshots of the foreground window (screenshot.py), recording and playing audio (microphone.py, local_audio_player.py), and managing conversation history. While the metadata contains highly unusual future-dated version numbers (e.g., certifi 2026.04.22) and references to non-existent AI models (e.g., GPT-5.4), the actual code logic is consistent with the add-on's stated purpose. It includes a transparent installation task that warns the user about the risks of AI controlling their mouse and keyboard and requires explicit consent. No evidence of malicious exfiltration of secrets or unauthorized background execution was found.
